plan_summary.primary_goal: Generate robust, attributable evidence over a 48-month controlled pilot to inform national labor-reform tooling on the 4-day work week.
plan_summary.modelling_frame: Tests the formal-track operations envelope against projected monthly program cost; the SME administrative-overhead margin against the 2 worker-hour/week cap; the voluntary-participation margin against the 75% threshold; and the post-incentive productivity-retention margin against the 75% sustained-efficiency target. State Labor Department binding sign-off, legal executive-notification viability, and union/political acceptance are treated as unmodelled existential gates.

key_values.label: Total project budget
key_values.comment: INR 2,000 crore total envelope across both tracks plus contingency; the central financing input for the 48-month pilot.
key_values.source_text: Total budget ceiling: INR 2,000 crore — input to total cost modeling.

key_values.label: Pilot duration
key_values.comment: Primary measurement window for the controlled pilot, including evaluation and national-toolkit transition.
key_values.source_text: Pilot period for primary measurement: 48 months — total pilot duration for Monte Carlo simulation horizon.

key_values.label: Formal-track budget share
key_values.comment: 70% of the INR 2,000 crore envelope is allocated to the formal-sector pilot track (operations plus incentives).
key_values.source_text: Formal sector 4DWW budget allocation: 70% — input to cost breakdown model.

key_values.label: Incentive share of the formal-track budget
key_values.comment: Within the formal-track envelope, 80% is earmarked for productivity-sharing grants; the remaining 20% funds program operations.
key_values.source_text: Incentive budget allocation favors productivity grants at 80% — input to budget split simulation.

key_values.label: SME administrative-overhead cap
key_values.comment: SME pilot compliance must stay at or below 2 worker-hours per week; exceeding it triggers the Risk-2 data-integrity failure mode.
key_values.source_text: SME data compliance target is less than 2 worker-hours per week — performance gate.

key_values.label: Voluntary opt-in participation target
key_values.comment: Voluntary opt-in commitment across the first 24 months must hold above 75% for the experimental design to remain valid.
key_values.source_text: Voluntary opt-in participation rates remain above seventy-five percent throughout the first twenty-four months.

key_values.label: Post-incentive productivity-retention target
key_values.comment: Sustained efficiency after the incentive period (Month 37+) must hold at 75% of in-incentive levels to claim structural rather than subsidised change.
key_values.source_text: If sustained productivity < 75% threshold, trigger formal review under Issue 1.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Monthly program operating cost across the pilot
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Required to test whether the 20% non-incentive slice of the formal-track envelope covers PMO, audits, hardware, and liaison-team operations across 48 months.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Bottom-up: PMO staffing × FTE rate + third-party audit contract value/period + SME hardware lifecycle + UMF/cloud hosting; cross-check against the INR 50 crore evaluation-loss figure named in Risk 2.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Projected SME compliance hours per week under deployed tooling
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Determines whether the low-friction tablet and visual-scale interface (Decision 5) hits the ≤2 hr/week target or breaches it and collapses the SME cohort.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Per Data Item 6: baseline ASI exercise on a sample SME group; per-task time-and-motion measurements scaled to the full weekly data-entry cadence.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Achieved voluntary opt-in share across the first 24 months
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Below the 75% target the cohort design loses statistical power and selection bias amplifies (Risk 4, Risk 5).
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Cohort-recruitment funnel: MOU signings / target cohort size, projected by city; sensitivity to union resistance per Risk 5.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Achieved post-incentive productivity retention
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Tests the central scaling claim that the 4DWW is structurally sustained, not subsidised; failure invalidates the national-toolkit recommendation.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Per Expert Issue 2: survival analysis across 10 historical subsidy programs to derive an evidence-based decay rate; assume 50% baseline decay with bounded sensitivity.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Total non-incentive program operating cost over the pilot
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Aggregates the monthly burn into a single 48-month pressure that can be netted against the formal-track operations envelope.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Formal-track operations budget surplus
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Tests whether the 20% non-incentive slice of the formal-track envelope (~INR 280 crore) is sufficient for PMO, audits, liaison-team, and hardware operations across 48 months.

recommended_first_calculations.label: SME admin-overhead margin against the 2 hr/week cap
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Positive margin means SMEs can comply without breaching the 2 hr/week ceiling; negative margin triggers the FM2 Data Integrity Collapse path identified in the premortem.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Voluntary participation margin against the 75% target
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Below zero the cohort loses statistical power and selection bias dominates; the M&E claim on national generalisability cannot be made.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Post-incentive productivity-retention margin
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Tests the structural-vs-subsidised claim; if retention falls below 75%, the national-toolkit recommendation rests on incentive dependency rather than durable productivity change.

unmodelled_gates.label: State Labor Departments accept binding PMO tie-breaking vote
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Premortem A1 and Expert Issue 1.6 name this as the single most critical authority assumption; without it the quarterly decision gates and rollback execution lack enforceable jurisdiction, regardless of any financial or operational margin the model evaluates.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Decision-gate paralysis; rollback execution stalls past two quarterly review cycles per Expert Criticism; central PMO cannot enforce standardised metrics or operational rollbacks across the federal/state split.

unmodelled_gates.label: Executive notification suffices for 4DWW workday redefinition across 24 months
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Expert Criticism flags reliance on minimal executive notification (rather than full legislative amendment) as a load-bearing legal assumption; if notifications fail or are challenged, the pilot's legal basis collapses without a legislative fallback inside the 12-month launch window.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Halting 10% of formal cohort pending clarification; INR 5 crore unplanned legal review; 4-week data collection pause; potential 3–6 month pilot delay per Risk 1.

unmodelled_gates.label: Unions remain cooperative and political climate stable across the 48-month pilot
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Premortem A7 and Risk 5 treat sustained union cooperation as binary; organized opposition, boycotts, or a political-cycle reversal would shut the pilot down independently of any productivity-retention margin the model evaluates.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Industrial disputes, boycotts stopping pilot operations, INR 10 crore crisis-communication spend per Risk 5; risk of forced 5-day reversion invalidating the structural-change objective.
